channel.title: Internship on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Internship on RaihnForge

item.title: ITAM Project Management Internship Reflection
item.description:
1. Learning Objectives

My Spring 2025 internship at an EdTech employer focused on three core learning objectives that guided my development as a systems-oriented project manager and visual communicator. Building on foundational experiences from ITAM 490A, I sought to translate theory into practice by enhancing tool proficiency, streamlining operational workflows, and elevating cross-team communication through visual storytelling.

Objective 1 — Become Proficient with Jira

I deepened my knowledge of Jira by managing sprint cycles, grooming backlogs, and setting up project-specific dashboards. These systems were implemented across both the internal content-system product pipeline and the Upper Elementary microlesson content initiative. I structured Epics and Stories to reflect evolving production workflows, enabling greater task traceability and faster throughput. Dashboards became key tools in daily team syncs, reinforcing accountability and aligning output with strategic objectives.

item.title: ITAM Internship A Reflection
item.description:
1. Learning Objectives

My first learning objective was to gain experience as an Agile project-management scrum master for the development team. Though I assist in prioritization and help unblock the development team throughout sprints, our team does not ascribe to strict scrum implementation. I also prefer to avoid the scrum points system, as it creates a distraction and an alluring incentive for team members to prioritize the gamified system over the actual applied outcomes. My objective was modified to increase confidence and organization during developer daily standups and bi-weekly meetings. I incorporated weekly one-on-one meetings with individual developers in order to assess and assist with progress and prioritization.

item.title: Three Facts, Three Questions
item.description: A note on naming. The original paper named the specific EdTech employer, its flagship product, and the device and productivity ecosystem the platform depends on. For public posting, all of these have been generalized: “the organization,” “the survey platform,” and “a major cloud productivity suite and device ecosystem.” The analysis is preserved. The intent was always portfolio reflection on the habit of pre-employment diligence, not commentary on any specific commercial entity.
